heroHeadline: TNLA Welcomes Junta Government, Fracturing Three Brotherhood Alliance; SAC Bombs Monastery and Kills Civilians During Thingyan; SCEF Resistance Coalition Formed
heroSubtitle: On April 15, the Palaung State Liberation Front/Ta'ang National Liberation Army (PSLF/TNLA) issued a statement welcoming Min Aung Hlaing's new government — a major political reversal attributed to Chinese diplomatic pressure, potentially fracturing the Three Brotherhood Alliance that launched Operation 1027. Simultaneously, the NUG, KIO, KNU, KNPP, and CNF announced the Steering Council for a Federal Democratic Union (SCEF), deepening resistance coalition governance. During Myanmar's Thingyan Water Festival (April 13–17), the SAC struck Seik Hkun Monastery in Sagaing (killing 2 monks, wounding 11), deployed gyrocopters that killed an infant in Salingyi and a 14-year-old girl in Yinmabin, burned ~200 homes in North Lukan village (Pakokku/Magway), continued three days of intensive airstrikes on Hpakant (Kachin), and killed 6 in Pauk Township on New Year's Day. Four unidentified bodies were found at Hpakant's Ma Shi Ka Htaung junction on April 14 following KIA-SAC fighting. In a symbolic gesture, the junta's Thingyan amnesty released RFA contributor Shin Daewe after 915 days — while simultaneously bombing civilians across Myanmar's heartland.
footerNote: Data sourced from AAPP, ACLED, UN OCHA, Irrawaddy, RFA, Myanmar Now, DVB, Al Jazeera, Mizzima, Human Rights Watch, Crisis Group, Myanmar Peace Monitor, MoeMaKa, IMNA/Mon News, FDD, and official NUG, KNU, KIA, and AA statements.
